mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances when unemployment rises above the specified threshold over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to yield positive forward returns in >50% of cases over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations during confirmed expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPI exceeds the threshold, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates robust labor market absorption and wage growth, supporting corporate earnings and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of monthly observations over a 10-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with expanded corporate profit margins and revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with subsequent 60-day equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production falls while retail sales rise signals inventory depletion and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: This divergence pattern is followed by positive returns in >50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when inventory ratios normalize.
